name: Bishnupriya Manipuri
notes: Bishnupriya Manipuri (বিষ্ণুপ্রিয়া মণিপুরী), an Indo-Aryan language of Assam/Tripura and Bangladesh (not to be confused with Meitei/Manipuri, which is Tibeto-Burman). STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Written in the Bengali–Assamese script, an abugida with an inherent vowel, conjuncts and matras; a full Bengali-script map would be required.

ancestors.notes: Indo-Aryan — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Bishnupriya Manipuri.
